data.title: Two Best Active ETFs to Consider in Today's Choppy Market
data.description:
Active ETFs have been gaining traction as investors look for more flexible and research-driven strategies that can adapt faster than traditional index funds. Currently, two funds continue to stand out: Natixis Loomis Sayles Focused Growth ETF (LSGR) and T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation ETF (TCAF). Both bring long track records in active management, and are top picks for investors who want more than simple market‑cap exposure.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

Natixis Loomis Sayles Focused Growth ETF (LSGR)

LSGR is an active U.S. large‑cap growth ETF run by the well‑known Loomis Sayles Growth Equity team. The fund aims for long‑term growth of capital and follows a high‑conviction, research‑heavy approach. Rather than copying a broad index, it builds a tight portfolio of about 20 to 25 high‑quality companies that trade below fair value.

Recent top holdings include Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), and Micron (MU). Overall, the ETF has $861.25 million in assets under management (AUM). Also, it has an expense ratio of 0.59%.

Turning to Wall Street, LSGR ETF has a Strong Buy consensus rating. Of the 21 stocks held, 20 have Buy ratings and one has a Hold rating. At $56.59, the average LSGR ETF price target implies a 32.43% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Space Stocks Get Smoked as SpaceX (SPCX) Profit Taking Wipes Out Pre-IPO Gains
data.description:
Space stocks surged earlier today, June 12, as investors rushed to find more ways to gain exposure to SpaceX (SPCX), the aerospace firm led by Elon Musk. The sharp price increase came hours before the company began trading publicly on Nasdaq (NDAQ) under the ticker SPCX. 

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Double your ASTS exposure with Tradr's ASTX

However, many space stocks are now declining sharply just a few hours after their debuts, as market momentum appears to be cooling. Notably, the earlier buying frenzy had pushed several space-linked firms such as EchoStar (SATS) and AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) much higher before the recent crash.

Investors Pile Into SpaceX Proxy Stocks, Sparking Rally

According to earlier reports, stocks with direct or indirect ties to SpaceX attracted massive investor interest before the rocket firm's market debut. Shares of EchoStar climbed roughly 11% on Thursday as investors focused on its estimated 3% stake in SpaceX. The stock added another 5% in premarket trading earlier on Friday.

Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile surged 12% as investors focused on the company's relationship with SpaceX. At the same time, aerospace tourism firm Virgin Galactic (SPCE) saw a sharp rise in options activity despite its previous price crash earlier this month. 

Space Stocks Reverse Course, Wiping Out Earlier Gains

Just a few hours after SpaceX officially began trading on Nasdaq, SPCX rose about 30% to over $176, then fell about 5% to $169 a few minutes later. During this volatile period, stocks in the aerospace sector declined sharply. 

Shares of Rocket Lab (RLBK) and Planet Labs PBC (PL) fell over 8% each, while Intuitive Machines (LUNR) declined by roughly 11%. Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile and EchoStar, which had risen earlier, both crashed by more than 12%. Additionally, space tourism firm Virgin Galactic's stock plummeted about 28% after rallying by over 20% on Thursday. 

Even ETFs tied to the sector have seen a sharp drop. Procure Space ETF (UFO), Roundhill Space & ‌Technology ⁠ETF (MARS), and Ark Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (BATS) saw a price decline between 1% and 8% after the SpaceX debut. 

The slump comes as short-term investors prioritize profit taking over long-term holding. Chris Beauchamp, the chief market analyst at fintech company IG Group, said: "The space sector has seen a strong run-up, and profit-taking is that lazy sort of excuse for why things have gone down." 

What Are the Best Space Stocks to Invest In? 

The best space stocks to buy depend on investors' preferences and analysts' insights. SpaceX (SPCX) is currently rated a Hold by Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks. Meanwhile, other space stocks, such as EchoStar (SATS), have been rated a Moderate Buy, and Intuitive Machines (LUNR) a Strong Buy. For more details on these stocks' price targets, ratings, and performance, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: U.S.-Iran Deal Odds Hit 80 to 85%, Senior U.S. Official Says
data.description:
A senior U.S. official told reporters on Friday that the odds of the U.S. and Iran signing a peace deal have risen to between 80% and 85%. That came after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that a memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the two countries has "never been closer."

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

"We feel very good about the agreement," said the official. "We're not at the finish line but we're very close to it."

Trump Expects Iran Deal by Monday

President Trump has also expressed optimism over progress in negotiations. He told Axios that he expects an agreement by Monday, June 15. On Polymarket, traders assign 45% odds of the U.S. announcing a ceasefire extension or new agreement with Iran by Monday.

Neither country has yet to release the terms of the agreement. However, reports have circulated that the MOU will include Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz. In return, the U.S. will lift its blockade on Iranian ports. Other reports have stated that Iran's benefits from the deal will be performance-based.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Enliven Therapeutics Stock (ELVN) Up Today?
data.description:
Enliven Therapeutics (ELVN) stock was up on Friday after the biopharmaceutical company announced an upsized offering. The clinical-stage biotech priced the offering at $37.50 per share. Investors were also given the option to buy pre-funded warrants that were priced at $37.499. In total, the company is selling about 8.93 million shares and 1.73 million warrants, all of which are being issued directly by Enliven.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The offering is expected to bring in roughly $400 million in gross proceeds before fees and expenses. Enliven also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.6 million shares, which could increase the total raise if exercised. The deal is set to close around June 15, 2026, assuming standard closing conditions are met.

"This financing strengthens our balance sheet as we continue advancing our pipeline of small molecule therapeutics," the company said in its announcement. The capital raise gives Enliven additional runway to fund clinical development. It will also use the money for general corporate needs. The company said that the $400 million in funding will support ongoing trials and future pipeline expansion.

Enliven Therapeutics Stock Movement Today

Enliven Therapeutics stock was up 16.03% on Friday, extending a 204.87% year-to-date rally. The stock has also climbed 84.21% over the past 12 months.

Investors will note that ELVN stock experienced heavy trading today. This saw some 4.4 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 751,000 shares.

Is Enliven Therapeutics Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Enliven Therapeutics is Strong Buy, based on nine Buy ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average ELVN stock price target of $59.63, suggesting a possible 28.89% upside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why Is Silver Up 6% Today, 6/12/26?
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD) futures are up by over 6% on Friday as the U.S. and Iran edge closer to a peace deal. However, the precious metal is still down by about 20% over the past month.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An end to the war will likely result in a recovery of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. That could send oil and gas prices lower and ease inflation expectations. With lower inflation, the odds of higher interest rates fall. That directly benefits silver because it doesn't pay out interest. In other words, the opportunity cost of holding silver relative to interest-bearing assets goes down.

U.S.-Iran Deal Inches Closer

President Trump told Axios that a deal could be signed by Monday. Iran is also optimistic, with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi saying this morning that an agreement has "never been closer."

Many silver investors were caught off guard by silver's decline after the war began on February 28. That's because silver is seen as a safe-haven asset and store of value. However, the prospect of higher rates outweighed its safe-haven appeal.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: AXT Stock (AXTI) Soars on an Indium Phosphide Shortage Report
data.description:
AXT (AXTI) stock was on the rise Friday alongside reports that China's slow approval of exports could cause supply issues. Specifically, this report focuses on indium phosphide. This is a critical component needed for the development of AI data centers. Indium phosphide substrates are used in the optical chips required for these AI data centers to operate. Without them, construction timelines for new data centers could face significant delays.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

AXTX: an alternative to margin or options on AXTI

This is where AXT comes into the picture and explains why the stock was up today. AXT is a design, development, production, and distribution company that handles compound and single-element semiconductor substrates. This includes the much-needed indium phosphide substrates for AI data centers. If there's going to be a shortage of indium phosphide, this would increase the demand for AXT's products. That could lead to strong revenue gains as AI data center builders pay a premium for its indium phosphide substrates.

How Today's News Has Affected AXTI Stock

AXT stock experienced a strong rally today, with shares up 16.07% as of this afternoon. The company's stock has also rocketed 516.54% year-to-date and has undergone a massive 3,989.81% rally over the past 12 months.

Despite today's price increase, trading activity for AXTI stock was muted. This saw some 6 million shares change hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume is well above that at about 9.9 million shares.

Is AXTI Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AXT is Moderate Buy, based on three Buy and two Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AXTI stock price target of $86. This represents a potential 15.98% upside for the shares. The stock may see updated analyst coverage in light of today's indium phosphide shortage report.
data.author: William White

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Rises as Iran Affirms Deal Progress; SpaceX Makes Debut
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Friday as optimism builds for a U.S.-Iran peace deal.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said a deal has "never been closer." He added that further details will be released soon.

"The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has never been closer," said Araghchi in an X post. "Pending its finalization, the media should refrain from entering speculation about its content." President Trump echoed his announcement by sharing Araghchi's statement in a Truth Social post. On Thursday, Trump paused planned strikes against Iran due to progress in negotiations.

Elsewhere, consumer sentiment staged a comeback in June. The University of Michigan's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment rose to 48.9, above the estimate of 46. The index fell to an all-time low of 44.8 in May as gas prices soared higher.

However, the average price of a gallon of gas has dropped to $4.10 from $4.50 over the past month. That helped lift sentiment while also deflating inflation expectations. "Lower-income consumers exhibited a particularly strong sentiment increase, consistent with the fact that gasoline comprises a larger share of their budgets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Year-ahead inflation expectations dropped to 4.6% from 4.8%. However, they were at 3.4% before the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28. Long-term inflation expectations fell to 3.4% from 3.9%.

Finally, SpaceX (SPCX) made its long-awaited debut on the Nasdaq (NDAQ) exchange. SPCX opened at $150, higher than its IPO price of $135. A total of 555.6 million shares were offered.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.52% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Robinhood Stock (HOOD) Rises on 'Record-Breaking Traffic' after SpaceX (SPCX) IPO
data.description:
Robinhood's (HOOD) shares edged higher on Friday afternoon after the online trading platform reported a "record-breaking traffic today." This came as retail investors raced to grab a slice of aerospace space and rocket firm SpaceX's (SPCX) public market debut on Nasdaq.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

SpaceX launched its long-awaited initial public offering (IPO) on Friday, raising $75 billion — the largest amount ever raised from such an offering in Wall Street's history.

The IPO — which crowned CEO Elon Musk as the world's first trillionaire — also gave SpaceX a valuation of just under $2 trillion. This made it the sixth-most valuable public company in the world, after Meta (META) and Tesla (TSLA).

SpaceX reserved 20% of its IPO shares for the retail market — trimmed from an earlier 30% — and Robinhood was one of online brokerage platforms that had access to the IPO offering. Others include Fidelity, Charles Schwab (SCHW), SoFi Technologies (SOFI) and Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*TRADE.

"Robinhood saw record-breaking traffic today," Robinhood announced on X on Friday afternoon, noting that its essential systems have recovered after "some customers experienced latency and intermittent issues."
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Sinks after Recall of Dangerous Dive Sticks for Kids
data.description:
Thousands of children's toys and swimming products sold at major retailers such as Walmart (WMT) and Amazon (AMZN) have been recalled because of the threat of serious physical injury. Walmart's stock was down in mid-trading.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

High conviction AMZN bears now have this Tradr ETF

Serious Piercing Injuries

Chinese manufacturer Joyin said it was recalling Sloosh Dive Sticks because they had violated a federal ban. This was, it said, because they exceeded the compress limit, posing an impalement hazard. In shallow water, children may fall or land on a dive stick, resulting in serious piercing injuries. Facial and eye injuries are also possible when children attempt to retrieve the sticks underwater.

The recall affects around 254,000 products that were sold prior to October, 2025.

A recall of LiKee Pull String teething toys has also been initiated by Chilan Tech because they violated the mandatory standard for toys. This is because the silicone strings are smaller and longer than permitted. The strings can reach the back of children's throat and become lodged, posing a serious risk of respiratory distress and deadly choking hazard.

A total of 24,000 toys, which were sold by Amazon and Walmart, are being recalled.

Key Walmart Risks

Although these children's products were not manufactured by Walmart, recalls such as these matter to the company because selling dangerous products can damage the company's finances and reputation. This is why the ability to sell is a key risk for both the company and retail investors when looking to add stocks to their portfolio– see below:
data.author: David Craik
